page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c2d server family. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using x86_64 architecture with simultaneous multithreading. The instance provides dedicated CPU allocation and is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include bundled local storage or GPUs, and offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core metrics placing in the top 25% at 2928.59. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 11730.12 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 6622.0, both falling in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth is stable, recording 553.05 GB/sec for small-block cached reads and 62.15 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported by hardware-accelerated instructions including AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

While the instance handles lighter LLM tasks adequately, achieving 240.29 tokens per second for text generation on a small 135M model, it exhibits a performance bottleneck on larger models, with prompt processing for a medium 7B model dropping to a weak 18.65 tokens per second. The lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs limits its utility for high-throughput database storage or heavy deep learning training. However, its high memory density and strong single-core execution make it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads such as Redis caching, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM, offering a high resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. It delivers 8.97 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 10.63 GB/sec in static web serving. However, LLM prompt processing for 7B models is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications, including in-memory databases, caching layers, software compilation, and general-purpose web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but lacks local storage and GPUs.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-8 is an x86_64 instance with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high memory density for caching and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores
Equipped with 64 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and web serving
